title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Seven: Which introduces the genie (Fragment 3)
text:
“I made a vow!” the face howled, in a large, windy roar. “The one who lets me out shall suffer. There !” The misty arms gestured.

The two men holding the cork and the bottle seemed to wink out of existence. Cork and bottle both fell to the ground, forcing the genie to billow sideways from the neck of the bottle. From the midst of his blue vapor, two large toads came crawling and seemed to gaze around in bewilderment. The genie came slowly and vaporously upright, hovering above the bottle with his smoky arms folded and a look of utter hatred on his misty face.

By this time everyone had run away except for Abdullah and Kabul Aqba, Abdullah because he could barely move in his chains and Kabul Aqba because he was clearly unexpectedly brave. The genie glowered at the two of them.

“I am the slave of the bottle,” he said. “Much as I hate and detest the whole arrangement, I have to tell you that he who owns me is allowed one wish every day and I am forced to grant it.” And he added menacingly, “What is your wish?”

“I wish—” began Abdullah.

Kabul Aqba quickly rammed his hand across Abdullah’s mouth. “ I am the one wishing,” he said. “Get that quite clear, genie!”

“I hear,” said the genie. “What wish?”

“One moment,” said Kabul Aqba. He put his face close to Abdullah’s ear. His breath smelled even worse than his hand, although neither, Abdullah had to admit, was a patch on Jamal’s dog. “Well, magician,” the bandit whispered, “you’ve proved you know what you’re talking about. Advise me what to wish and I’ll make you a free man and an honored member of my band. But if you try to make a wish yourself, I kill you. Understand?” He put the muzzle of his pistol to Abdullah’s head and let go of his mouth. “What shall I wish?”

“Well,” said Abdullah, “the wisest and kindest wish would be to wish your two toads turned back into men.”

Kabul Aqba spared a surprised glance for the two toads. They were crawling uncertainly along the muddy edge of the pool, obviously wondering whether they could swim or not. “A waste of a wish,” he said. “Think again.”

Abdullah racked his brain for what might please a bandit chief most. “You could ask for limitless wealth, of course,” he said, “but you would then need to carry your money, so perhaps you should first wish for a team of sturdy camels. And you would need to defend this treasure. Perhaps your first wish should be for a supply of the famous weapons of the north, or—”

“But which ?” demanded Kabul Aqba. “Hurry. The genie is becoming impatient.”

This was true. The genie was not exactly tapping his foot, since he had no feet to tap, but there was something about his looming, lowering blue face that suggested there would be two more toads by the pool if he had to wait much longer.

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Seven: Which introduces the genie

vocabulary.definition: An agreement or plan made between parties.
vocabulary.example: I hate and detest the whole arrangement.

vocabulary.definition: A robber or outlaw belonging to a gang.
vocabulary.example: The bandit whispered to the magician.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of being very puzzled or confused.
vocabulary.example: The toads seemed to gaze around in bewilderment.

vocabulary.definition: To swell out or puff up, especially by the action of the wind.
vocabulary.example: The genie billowed sideways from the neck of the bottle.

vocabulary.definition: Courageous and willing to face danger or pain.
vocabulary.example: Kabul Aqba was clearly unexpectedly brave.

vocabulary.definition: Metal fasteners used to restrain movement.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah could barely move in his chains.

vocabulary.definition: To protect from harm or danger.
vocabulary.example: You would need to defend this treasure.

vocabulary.definition: To dislike intensely; hate.
vocabulary.example: I hate and detest the whole arrangement.

vocabulary.definition: To make a movement with a part of the body to express meaning or feeling.
vocabulary.example: The misty arms gestured.

vocabulary.definition: To look at someone angrily or sullenly.
vocabulary.example: The genie glowered at the two of them.

vocabulary.definition: To remain suspended in one place in the air.
vocabulary.example: Hovering above the bottle with misty face.

vocabulary.definition: To utter a loud, protracted, doleful cry, as animals (e.g., wolves) do.
vocabulary.example: The face howled in a large, windy roar.

vocabulary.definition: Easily irritated or annoyed by delay.
vocabulary.example: The genie was becoming impatient.

vocabulary.definition: Without any limit; infinite.
vocabulary.example: You could ask for limitless wealth.

vocabulary.definition: Appearing as a vague form, often large and threatening.
vocabulary.example: His looming, lowering blue face suggested danger.

vocabulary.definition: In a threatening manner.
vocabulary.example: He added menacingly, “What is your wish?”

vocabulary.definition: The projecting part of the face including the nose and mouth; also the end of a firearm's barrel.
vocabulary.example: He put the muzzle of his pistol to Abdullah’s head.

vocabulary.definition: To be far inferior to something else.
vocabulary.example: Neither was a patch on Jamal's dog.

vocabulary.definition: To cause extreme physical or mental suffering; to torment.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah racked his brain for what might please a bandit chief.

vocabulary.definition: To push or drive forcibly into something.
vocabulary.example: Kabul Aqba quickly rammed his hand across Abdullah’s mouth.

vocabulary.definition: Strongly and solidly built.
vocabulary.example: You should first wish for a team of sturdy camels.

vocabulary.definition: The quality of being complete or absolute.
vocabulary.example: A look of utter hatred on his misty face.

vocabulary.definition: A substance diffused or suspended in the air, especially one normally liquid or solid.
vocabulary.example: From the midst of his blue vapor, two large toads came crawling.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner resembling or consisting of vapor; indistinctly or indistinctively.
vocabulary.example: The genie came slowly and vaporously upright.

vocabulary.definition: A solemn promise or assertion; a pledge.
vocabulary.example: "I made a vow!" the face howled.
